quotes.verbatim_quote:
The main chords in tunes may be converged on or embellished
with other chords that may precede or lead up to them. Whatever
chord you are leading up to (main chord) is or can be usually pre-
ceded by a five chord with a tritone. This interval participates
in this five or dominant-seventh chord as well as another seventh
chord a flatted 5th from its root.
It is just a matter of using chomatic tones to resolve the tritone
quotes.why_load_bearing: Core technique rule for applying scales over dominant seventh chords.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Scale #3 has a major 3rd already in it so it has to used as above
to embellish the 5th of the F minor.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Practical rule for handling scale-key conflict.
